findingDetails.title: CUT self-recommendation guard remains live behind an environment flag
findingDetails.evidence: 003 says CUT/delete the self-guard and constants say the explicit opt-in guard was removed, but fusion.ts still exports the env flag, reads it, and branches on shouldApplyAdvisorSelfRecommendationGuard in confidenceFor and primaryIntentBonus.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove the guard flag/branches or amend specs/constants to own dormant retention and add flag-on coverage.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Grep found the flag and guard branches in fusion.ts while 003 docs and constants claim CUT/removed semantics.

noveltyJustification: First iteration against an empty registry; one new P1 with direct spec-vs-code contradiction evidence.
traceabilityChecks: fusion flag and guard branches checked
edgeCases: LEAF agent can execute only one iteration per dispatch; owning /deep:review loop must dispatch remaining angles
ruledOut: unflagged auditRecsAdvisorPenalty is intended remaining defense, not itself a finding
WS1 arithmetic fix is out of scope for angle 1

focus: WS1 empirically falsified claim has no evidence trail

findingDetails.title: WS1 falsification is claimed at the parent but absent from the owning child packet
findingDetails.evidence: The parent says 001's WS1 arithmetic fix was empirically falsified/re-scoped, while 001 remains Planned / Not started — GATED, says no verification has run, and keeps WS1 implementation, re-run, and baseline checklist items unchecked. The only reconstructed experiment summary is commit e2711fb580; the owning packet does not record it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add an owning 001 close-out/deviation record with the WS1 experiment inputs/results/revert, or amend the parent until that packet-local evidence exists.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Search/read across parent spec and 001 spec, implementation summary, tasks, checklist, and research note showed parent/charter falsification claim but child pending/GATED state with no falsification record.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 evidence-trail/spec-status mismatch added to one prior active P1; weighted new 5 over weighted total 10.
edgeCases: git commit message provides experiment summary but not packet-local file evidence
WS1 arithmetic fix not re-proposed per charter watch-out
ruledOut: current audit phrase calibration is not itself the defect
angle 3 live saturation audit deferred

focus: The saturation defect is still live

findingDetails.title: Surviving explicit-lane disambiguation penalties are still absorbed before fusion
findingDetails.evidence: Review/webflow/benchmark/resume disambiguation penalties still feed the same summed explicit score, emitted via Math.min(value.score, 1), then fusion indexes matches via Math.max from zero, erasing net-negative evidence and allowing positive verbose support to absorb demotions.
findingDetails.recommendation: Do not add more pre-clamp offsets; either document these as best-effort with fixture evidence or add a scoped post-cap/first-class demotion mechanism for supported disambiguators.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Audited angle-named penalty families in explicit.ts and verified each reaches the same explicit emit and fusion buildLaneMatchIndex floor path.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 added to two prior active P1 findings; weighted new 5 over weighted total 15.
edgeCases: does not re-propose the broad falsified WS1 fix
structural evidence only; no runtime corpus run performed
ruledOut: not claiming every affected prompt currently misroutes
corpus-number reconciliation deferred to angle 4

noveltyJustification: No new findings; the reviewed corpus numbers reconcile as distinct measurement regimes.
edgeCases: numbers reconciled from committed docs/tests/baseline JSON rather than rerunning scorer suites
149 vs 150 is a seeded provider ablation, not a production scorer improvement claim
ruledOut: corpus-number contradiction
treating pythonCorrect/tsAlsoCorrect as TS top-1 accuracy

focus: WS3 parity ledger has no owning child

findingDetails.title: WS3 parity acceptance lives in tests, while the owning packet still marks it unchecked
findingDetails.evidence: 001 declares WS3 as a P0 parity deliverable, but 001 tasks/checklist remain unchecked while parity tests assert the active four reviewed-accepted IDs and 193-row corpus.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add an owning WS3 close-out/decision record or update 001 tasks/checklist/status with the final four accepted IDs, 197-to-193 rename evidence, and exact parity gates.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked 001 requirement/tasks/checklist, parity tests, legacy corpus parity, local-native divergence fixture, and 005 summary; enforcement exists in tests but no completed owning WS3 packet record was found.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 added to three prior active P1 findings; weighted new 5 over weighted total 20.
edgeCases: parity gate may be functionally correct; finding is missing ownership/traceability
parity tests not rerun due read-only evidence scope and known suite failures
ruledOut: 105/101/4 contradiction
new correctness finding against the four accepted IDs

findingDetails.title: Executor-delegation tests assert only top-1, leaving synthesized recommendation metadata unpinned
findingDetails.evidence: The synthesized executor path creates a recommendation when the executor was absent, using fixed confidence/uncertainty, prior topScore, empty laneContributions, and null dominantLane; tests and fixtures pin top-1 routing, branch presence, and TS/Python parity but not these metadata semantics.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a focused injection-if-absent test that asserts reason, score policy, lane attribution fields, confidence/uncertainty, and threshold status for the synthesized executor recommendation.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Read the synthesized path, post-fusion integration, type contract, and all executor-delegation tests/fixtures. Coverage pins branch/top-1 behavior but not laneContributions, dominantLane, score, or fixed confidence/uncertainty fields on injected executor recommendations.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 added to four prior active P1 findings; weighted new 1 over weighted total 21.
edgeCases: post-abstention execution appears intentional and was not escalated without a concrete misroute
hardcoded 0.95 and 0.88 constants are documented route-lift/cap values; no requirement was found that they must be metadata-derived
ruledOut: P1 for post-abstention resurrection; implementation order and delegation cue semantics support current behavior
P1 for synthesized empty attribution; no failing consumer or incorrect route was proven

findingDetails.title: RRF exact-semantic rerank is implemented as a pairwise sort comparator instead of a transitive rank key
findingDetails.evidence: The RRF comparator applies command penalties, primary-intent bonuses, graph-conflict demotions, and exact-semantic reranking inside Array.sort. Exact-semantic rerank is only applied when each compared pair has exact entries and the pairwise adjusted-score gap is inside the window, while returned recommendations retain pre-adjustment score/confidence/attribution fields.
findingDetails.recommendation: Precompute a deterministic ranking tuple before sorting and expose or trace the adjusted ranking components so result metadata matches rank order.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed scoreAdvisorPrompt recommendation construction, RRF comparator, exact-semantic window, conflict adjustment, primary intent bonus path, and 003 GRADUATE evidence. Test grep found RRF determinism and corpus parity tests but no direct comparator-transitivity or emitted-score/adjusted-rank contract test for the exact-semantic window.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 added to four prior P1 findings and one prior P2 advisory; weighted new 5 over weighted total 26.
edgeCases: RRF remains default-off today, but the owning packet recommends graduation of the same path
finding does not disprove the reported 42-prompt benchmark lift
ruledOut: duplicate self-recommendation guard finding from iteration 1
benchmark-result regression claim; no reproduced top-1 failure in the 42-prompt set was reviewed

findingDetails.title: RRF graduation remains parked as a recommendation without a parent-tracked execution owner
findingDetails.evidence: 003 recommends graduating SPECKIT_ADVISOR_RRF_FUSION with the conflict seam, but fusion.ts still defaults the flag off; the same benchmark says live conflict value is absent until conflicts_with edges are authored, while the parent marks 003 complete and does not track RRF rollout/conflict authoring in open questions. The research charter still calls the GRADUATE/CUT verdicts unexecuted.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add an explicit parent-level follow-up row/open question or decision record naming the owner and state for RRF graduation: execute, defer, or require a post-WS2/WS4/WS5 rerun plus conflict-edge authoring criteria before promotion.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked the live flag default, 003 benchmark verdict/caveat, parent phase map/open questions, 004 follow-up scope, and research charter. The self-guard CUT has a follow-up packet, but RRF graduation/conflict-edge authoring remains visible only as recommendation/research rather than a parent-tracked execution decision.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 added to five prior P1 findings and one prior P2 advisory; weighted new 1 over weighted total 27.
edgeCases: did not rerun the 42-prompt benchmark; reviewed execution ownership and documented state
RRF default-off lowers severity because no default-on runtime regression was proven
self-guard CUT not re-reported because iteration 1 carries the code contradiction and 004 documents a follow-up path
ruledOut: new P1 for self-guard CUT ownership; 004 owns the production-default penalty contract
P1 for RRF default-off alone; 003 presents graduation as a recommendation and scopes live flip as a separate decision

findingDetails.title: 002 is rolled up as complete while child gates and the surviving fold-tick artifact remain incomplete
findingDetails.evidence: 002 and the 012 parent mark the runtime subtree complete, but 002's own phase map lists partial/no-go children and says parent status changes only after child strict validation passes. The 007 correction says the fold-tick script remains but its store/rerank dependencies were deleted after measured NO-GO, and the live script exits when the compiled fold core is missing.
findingDetails.recommendation: Change the 002/012 roll-up to partial or complete-with-deferred/no-go children and either remove/disable the orphan fold-tick script or add an explicit tombstone/NO-GO owner proving it is intentionally retained but non-runnable.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed 012 parent phase map, 002 parent status/transition rules, child status rows, 003 partial status, 004 shadow-only/pending status, 007 correction, and the surviving fold-tick script. The contradiction spans parent roll-up, child packet truth, and a live script surface.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 added to five prior P1 findings and two prior P2 advisories; weighted new 5 over weighted total 32.
traceabilityChecks: 002 parent and 012 parent complete statuses checked
edgeCases: 007 correction documents an operator decision not to revive outcome-weighted ranking, lowering the issue from runtime correctness to traceability/release-readiness
grep surfaced worktree copies of the script; active finding cites only the main workspace script and current 012/002 packet evidence
ruledOut: P0/P1 runtime crash on recommend path; the script header says cron/maintenance only and no prompt-time import was reviewed
stale-prose dismissal of the 007 correction; it matches current script dependency evidence

focus: Test coverage gaps on always-on hardcoded routing

findingDetails.title: Hardcoded routing overrides are covered unevenly and lack a direct branch inventory test
findingDetails.evidence: primaryIntentBonus contains many always-on regex overrides, with adjacent readOnlyRouteAllowed, low-info and Class-C gates. Some slices have direct tests, but grep did not surface direct fixture coverage for multiple branches named in the charter, including read-only allowlists, low-info/Class-C abstention shapes, and the code-audit/deep-review penalty pair; the deep-review literal check remains present beside the live deep-loop-workflows id without a direct branch test.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a table-driven unit suite enumerating every hardcoded routing branch with positive and negative fixtures, expected top candidate, confidence/uncertainty direction, and an explicit dead-id regression for deep-review versus deep-loop-workflows.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Audited primaryIntentBonus, readOnlyRouteAllowed, low-info and Class-C gates, then grepped scorer tests for direct branch names and representative prompts. Found direct tests for some branches but not a complete branch inventory or table-driven fixture that pins every always-on hardcoded route adjustment.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 added to six prior P1 findings and two prior P2 advisories; weighted new 1 over weighted total 33.
edgeCases: did not rerun the full scorer suite; this was a coverage inventory review
broad corpus/parity suites may cover end-to-end outcomes, so severity remains P2
deep-review literal may intentionally cover a canonical alias in some projections, but no direct branch fixture was found
ruledOut: duplicate P1 for self-recommendation guard; iteration 1 carries the active guard-code contradiction
runtime failure for low-info/Class-C gates; no failing prompt was executed or proven
